definition.title: One Home: Resort on the Road
definition.objective: Position LineHaul Station as a nationwide living network for drivers through the One Home program. Build 'Resort on the Road' as THE dominant content pillar. Feed creative into GA #1 ad campaign for testing. Must feel premium, aspirational, not pitchy. Communicate lifestyle upgrade, financial advantage, and network concept.
definition.target_audience: Truck drivers (primary — lifestyle aspiration + dignity), young/prospective drivers (mentorship track, entrepreneurial mindset), trucker families, carriers evaluating driver retention, minimalist lifestyle drivers
definition.cadence: ACTIVE NOW. First batch of lifestyle videos in production. GA #1 ad test imminent. Jeff viral short scripting next. Sleeper Makeover sub-concept in development.

top_level_summary: The LineHaul Station One Home program is designed to give drivers a true sense of home while living life on the road. Rather than referring to a 'home base,' which can sound overly military or operational, the concept centers around a Home Hub. The Home Hub is the driver's primary location within the LineHaul Station network. At their Home Hub, drivers may have a mailbox, personal vehicle parking (covered or garage), and a storage unit. These elements make that location uniquely theirs. However, the broader concept of One Home is that every LineHaul Station location delivers the same high-quality experience, so wherever a driver travels within the network, it still feels like home. As the LineHaul Station network expands across the country, each location will offer the same amenities, design standards, and overall atmosphere. The vision is to create a nationwide environment that feels more like a resort-style residential community for drivers than a traditional truck stop. Each location is designed to be beautiful, relaxing, and comfortable, with features such as high-end showers, spa and pool areas, carefully designed lighting, and premium shared spaces. The experience should feel consistently elevated compared to the typical options drivers have today. In this lifestyle model, the sleeper cab of the truck becomes the driver's bedroom, and LineHaul Station can enhance that experience through a Sleeper Makeover or Sleeper Customization program. This may include upgraded mattresses, mattress toppers, premium bedding, comfortable pillows, decor, and entertainment options to create a more personalized and comfortable sleeping environment. Another key advantage of the One Home program is financial flexibility. Traditional home ownership often requires paying for 365 days of housing expenses whether the home is used or not. Many drivers, however, may only use a home for a portion of the year. One Home allows drivers to pay only for the number of days they actually plan to use within the LineHaul Station network. Drivers can start with a smaller number of days — such as 60 days per year — and increase their membership incrementally over time. As the network grows and drivers find themselves using LineHaul Station locations more frequently, they can add additional days whenever they choose. Each increase adds to their refundable membership position rather than requiring a large upfront purchase or financing with interest. Over time, many drivers may expand their usage to 200 to 250 days per year, though they are never required to reach full-year usage. This flexibility allows drivers to grow their participation gradually as the network expands and their needs evolve. The goal of One Home is to provide drivers with a nationwide home network that is flexible, financially efficient, and significantly more comfortable than traditional options currently available on the road.

asset_readiness.summary: All campaign assets are currently in production or development. There are NO finished assets ready for social media calendar deployment at this time. This campaign cannot supply content to the 90-day planner until production is complete.

campaign_card.target_audience: Truck drivers (primary — lifestyle aspiration + dignity), young/prospective drivers (mentorship + entrepreneurial mindset), trucker families, carriers evaluating driver retention, minimalist lifestyle drivers
campaign_card.campaign_name: One Home: Resort on the Road
key_messages: One Home — a nationwide living network for drivers. Choose your Home Hub, and the entire LineHaul Station network becomes your home.
$13/day when you use it. Pay only for the days you need — no mortgage, no interest, no wasted rent on empty apartments.
Your sleeper is your bedroom. LineHaul is your home. Same comfort, same amenities, same experience — nationwide.

cta.lead_capture_point: One Home Lifestyle Calculator landing page → membership registration

_legacy_card.description: CONTENT IN PRODUCTION. Dominant content pillar. One Home is a nationwide living network for drivers — choose a Home Hub, and the entire LineHaul Station network becomes home. Same comfort, same amenities, same experience at every location. The only things tied to Home Hub: mailbox, personal vehicle parking, storage unit. Everything else is identical everywhere. Drivers pay only for the days they use, starting as low as 60 days/year and growing in any increment up to 365. No mortgage, no interest, fully refundable membership. Sleeper Makeover sub-concept brings bedroom customization (mattress, linens, pillows, decor, audio/video) under the One Home umbrella. ALL ASSETS CURRENTLY WIP — NONE READY FOR 90-DAY CALENDAR.
_legacy_card.strategic_purpose: Build 'Resort on the Road' and 'One Home' as THE dominant content pillar. Feed creative into GA #1 ad campaign for testing. Aspirational lifestyle positioning — premium, not pitchy. Communicate: lifestyle upgrade, financial advantage, network concept, flexible membership model. Position LHS as a nationwide resort-style living system for drivers.

_legacy_card.pre_approved_angles: One Home nationwide living network. Home Hub (NOT 'home base'). Resort on the Road. The Rig Carlton. $13/day financial comparison ($13 vs $50 apartment vs $110 hotel vs $65 truck stop). Pay only for days you use. Flexible membership growth (60 → 120 → 200 → 365 days). No mortgage, no interest. Fully refundable membership. Young driver entrepreneurship (skip the apartment, buy a truck). Reinvention / career change narrative. Minimalist lifestyle (sold everything). Sleeper Makeover. AI-generated resort experience visuals. 'Wherever the road takes you, you're already home.'
_legacy_card.artlist_cost: Sora2 prompts for AI character videos (4 scripts written — generation pending). Long-form ad production ongoing (Artlist footage + renderings). Viral shorts need Jeff filming time. New renderings pending from Architect Ron. Logo/branding design work underway.
_legacy_card.repurposing: FUTURE (when assets are ready): Long-form ads → social clips, reels, story cuts. Sora2 character videos → individual platform posts (Instagram reels, LinkedIn stories, Facebook video). Financial math → static graphics, carousel breakdowns. Voiceover scripts → audio snippets, caption copy, quote graphics. Jeff explainer → educational clips, podcast seed content. Rendering imagery → static posts, carousel tours, story backgrounds. Lifestyle Calculator → screenshot walkthroughs, before/after financial comparisons. Sleeper Makeover → before/after content, product showcase posts.
_legacy_card.timeline: ACTIVE IN PRODUCTION — NOTHING CALENDAR-READY. Commercial ad version and Jeff explainer in production. Sora2 character scripts written and awaiting generation. New renderings pending from Architect Ron. Logo/branding work underway. Lifestyle Calculator landing page planned. Sleeper Makeover concept in development.
_legacy_card.language_rules: ALWAYS use 'Home Hub' — NEVER use 'home base' or 'base' (sounds military/operational). The term is personal and network-oriented. Example: 'Choose your Home Hub — and the entire LineHaul Station network becomes your home.' Other approved terms: One Home, Home Hub, Home Address, Home Port, Home Location, Home Station.
